prompts: The graph of a simple harmonic motion of a spring oscillator is typically a sinusoidal wave showing displacement versus time. On the vertical axis, it shows displacement from the equilibrium position, while the horizontal axis represents time. The sine or cosine wave illustrates key features of simple harmonic motion: amplitude (maximum displacement), period (time for one complete cycle), and frequency (number of cycles per unit time). The motion is periodic and oscillates symmetrically around the equilibrium position, with the restoring force proportional and opposite to the displacement, reflecting Hooke's Law.
